title: grastidium exaltatum
intro: Grastidium exaltatum, also known as the tall rat's-tail grass, is a species of grass native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Grastidium exaltatum include the relief of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Grastidium exaltatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves for its medicinal and health benefits.
